bottles.name: Dragonette Seven
bottles.notes:
I didn't anticipate that our first bottle experience on KazmiCellars.com would be one where I wasn't actually drinking wine. But here we are.
It's the night before Mother's Day. I'm fresh off a hip replacement, on enough medication to sedate a large animal, and Rachel has been an absolute rock through all of it — taking care of me without complaint, without hesitation. The least I could do was open something special for her.
Dragonette is our oldest standing wine membership we still hold from Santa Barbara. It means something to us. So the 2018 Syrah was an easy choice — it's one of Rachel's favorites and honestly I don't think we could have done better.
This isn't your traditional big, brooding Syrah. Think medium-plus bodied, elegant more than powerful. We noticed plum, a little blueberry, a subtle spice that weaves through without ever taking over. Nothing heavy, nothing aggressive. Just a beautifully composed wine that's had just enough time in the bottle to find itself. The 2018 vintage was a perfect call.
It paired beautifully with lamb shank. Let it breathe for about 45 minutes to an hour beforehand. Trust me on that. The wine opens up and the pairing becomes something else entirely.
I did manage to steal one sip. It made me extremely jealous of Rachel. Worth every drop.
## Who Should Grab This

If you love Syrah but find the big Napa style reds a little too much, Dragonette is your answer. Approachable, refined, and the kind of bottle you open when the moment deserves it.

## The Kazmi Take

**A membership we'll never give up. The 2018 is drinking beautifully right now. Don't wait too long on it. Happy Mother's Day, babe. 🍷**

bottles.name: Luigi Tecce Poliphemo
bottles.notes:
What do you do four or five days before major surgery? If you're me, you call your closest friends and head to your favorite restaurant. No fanfare, no big announcement. Just good people, a great table, and the understanding that a night like this deserves a wine worth remembering.
I arrived at La Stella Italian Steakhouse and Music Lounge in Addison about thirty minutes early and found my way to the bar. I asked for Garrett, my favorite sommelier, a guy who has never steered me wrong. I told him exactly what I was looking for. A few weeks back I had an incredible Rhône blend from Sardinia that stopped me cold and I couldn't for the life of me remember what it was. I said find me something I wouldn't typically order. A grape I wouldn't reach for on my own. A region I haven't explored. He didn't hesitate.
He came back with the 2018 Luigi Tecce Poliphemo from Campania, Italy. An Aglianico. Now the first time I ever had Aglianico was at Kenneth Volk in Santa Barbara, and I fell in love with the grape then. But a Campanian Aglianico? I had never gone there. Garrett told me there was only one catch, it needed an hour, maybe two, to open up properly. No problem. He opened it, decanted it, and kept me company with a little Burgundy in the meantime.
By the time our group sat down, ordered, laughed, and settled into the kind of conversation that only happens with people who really know you, the Poliphemo was doing its thing. Opening up slowly, like a bouquet coming to life in the glass. When Garrett finally poured it at the table it stopped everyone mid sentence. My crew is used to Silver Oak, Caymus, maybe a Prisoner or two. Good wines, great wines even. But when you're with me I don't cherry pick the familiar. I go for the discovery. And Garrett delivered.
Smoke. Leather. Texture. Tannins that were soft but absolutely lingering. It had weight without being heavy, complexity without being complicated. Paired with steak it was nothing short of perfect. Aglianico was made for a hearty dish and this one proved it beyond any doubt.


##Who Should Grab This: 

Anyone who thinks they've figured out Italian reds and wants to be proven beautifully wrong. If you love big structured reds but want something that tells a story most people at the table have never heard, this is your bottle.


##The Kazmi Take

** Garrett, you were on point. This one goes straight onto the wish list and one day it's coming home to the Kazmi Cellar. Don't sleep on Campania, this Aglianico will give Barolo a run for its money. As last suppers go, this one was worthy. 🍷**
